mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment reflect expanding aggregate labor income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:US unemployment rate fell to 4.3% by May 2017 indicating tight labor market
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months with positive PAYEMS growth are not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises borrowing costs and discount rates sufficiently to compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed raised rates in Dec 2016 and Mar 2017 with forward guidance indicating further hikes
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS > 1.0% over any 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 5.0% and the federal funds rate increases over a 3-month window, the interaction of tight labor markets and active monetary tightening restricts liquidity disproportionately, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and FEDFUNDS increased over prior 3 months
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling regression window.

mechanism_narrative: During low-inflation mid-cycle expansions, month-over-month increases in real retail sales signal resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive RSAFS growth do not generate statistically significant positive 5-day S&P 500 returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) when CPIAUCSL trailing 12-month change is below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction activity, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation raises nominal discount rates and squeezes real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases above 0.2% do not produce significantly negative 5-day S&P 500 returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing and utilities output, which supports aggregate corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in INDPRO fail to associate with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns with rank correlation greater than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, extremely tight labor markets trigger wage-push inflation fears and constrain future earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reached 4.3% in May 2017, below historical full-employment estimates
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE < 4.5% over any 2-year walk-forward period.
